summary: EXP-192 closes the moderate charitability-tension hole. The Yang & Wang (2002) timing critique now opens with explicit charitability framing: "Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument, the reported timing contradicts the shielding interpretation that kappa requires." This makes the argument structure clean: we accept -6.5 uGal arguendo for the ratio arithmetic, then show that even under the charitable assumption the TIMING of the signal (valleys at first/last contact, per Yang & Wang 2002 — the observer's own reanalysis) contradicts shielding (which would peak at maximum lunar coverage). The "you can't both use the value and reject it" objection is preempted.
